title: BLUELIGHT RAT: Periodic Screen Capture
description: Detects rapid JPEG file creation by non-image processes. BLUELIGHT captures screenshots at regular intervals and saves them as .jpg files before staging for exfiltration via OneDrive. High frequency (>3 per minute) indicates automated capture.
query: ('Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Events' or 'Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Operational Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'SOC Windows Sysmon Logs') and (('Event ID' = '11' and 'Target Filename' like '*.jpg') and not (('Process Name' like '*\\chrome.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\msedge.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\firefox.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\Photos.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\SnippingTool.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\mspaint.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\Photoshop.exe'))) | timestats span=1minute count as Screenshots by 'Process Name' | where Screenshots > 3
falsepositives: Camera or scanning software saving rapid captures
splunk_original: index=sysmon EventCode=11 TargetFilename="*.jpg" NOT Image IN ("*chrome.exe","*msedge.exe","*Photos.exe","*SnippingTool.exe") | bucket _time span=1m | stats count by _time, Image, TargetFilename | where count > 3
